meta.setting: Hoopa Valley approaches, Klamath-Trinity mountain roads, pack trails, and freight camps
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with pack travel, trade pressure, land rights, animal handling, and hard choices
meta.quality_bar: hand-cut, grounded, respectful, table-useful, western-only language

research_grounding.title: Hoopa Valley Tribe
research_grounding.use: Hoopa Valley tribal government, sovereignty, culture, traditions, natural resources, and future generations.

research_grounding.title: Karuk Tribe
research_grounding.use: Northern California river, natural resource, stewardship, and self-governance context.

research_grounding.title: Yurok Tribe
research_grounding.use: Sovereign rights, land and water stewardship, cultural preservation, reciprocity, and dignity.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Hoopa Valley Packers cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented freight note, trail warning, or pack-line observation generated later; do not use fake Native language
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Valley Freight Kin
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing packers, haulers, guides, and negotiators moving goods through hard country
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four grounded signs, strengths, or social facts
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate them as skilled freight and community workers with sovereignty, family obligations, and natural-resource knowledge.
Tie future moves to pack routes, animal handling, trade negotiation, safe lodging, route denial, lost freight, official pressure, and knowing when a load is worth refusing.
Make the page useful for player negotiations, rough travel, supply problems, and jurisdictional tension.
Do not turn them into sidekicks, wilderness stereotypes, or decorative guides.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Their page should feel like a load balanced so well the mule resents nobody but the hill. Freight is politics when the trail crosses land other men pretend is empty.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A packer scene should ask who owns the road, who needs the freight, what the animal knows, and what the riders must promise before the load moves.
must_preserve: Use respectful 1899 western and regional language only.
Do not invent sacred practices, fake words, or mystical traits.
Represent Native people as living families, workers, stewards, negotiators, and political actors.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/hoopa-valley-packers.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not reduce Native people to victims, guides, costumes, or symbols
do not use modern slang, modern gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is respectful, grounded, and useful for freight and travel play.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to 1899 Jefferson State western play and respects living Native communities.
